title: EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook (STEO) January 2026: Renewable Diesel and SAF Forecasts
content: The EIAâ€™s January 2026 Short-Term Energy Outlook forecasts continued growth in U.S. renewable diesel production, projected to average 250,000 barrels per day (b/d) in 2026 and rise to 290,000 b/d in 2027. Renewable diesel consumption is expected to follow this trend, averaging 220,000 b/d in 2026 and expanding to 260,000 b/d in 2027. The U.S. is anticipated to remain a net exporter of renewable diesel, with net imports holding steady at -20,000 b/d (indicating net exports) through 2027. For the "other biofuels" categoryâ€”which includes sustainable aviation fuel (SAF), renewable heating oil, and renewable naphthaâ€”production is forecast to average 40,000 b/d in 2026, increasing to 50,000 b/d in 2027. Consumption of these fuels is projected to match production levels, while net imports remain at zero, suggesting domestic supply will effectively meet demand.
author: Analysts Account
